_description: ModalAI Starling 2 hi-resolution color camera (M0107 IMX412 with M12 120-deg lens). Used as the source of truth for camera intrinsics, sensor geometry, and mount transform when synthesizing imagery from SITL flight data.
_source: https://docs.modalai.com/starling-2-datasheet  (Starling 2 C26 config: IMX412 + TOF + dual AR0144)
https://docs.modalai.com/M0161  (identical optical block, used for cross-checking specs)
_notes: Published FOV (120.4 / 93.5 deg) is for the as-built lens including barrel distortion.
Geometric pinhole FOV computed from sensor + focal length differs (DFOV ~111 deg, HFOV ~98.7 deg, VFOV ~82.2 deg).
Use 'fov_deg' (manufacturer values) for footprint estimates and EXIF; use 'pinhole' block for projection math.
Distortion coefficients are unknown from public docs; left as zeros for an ideal-pinhole approximation. Replace with calibrated values if available.

mount_transform here is the nominal forward-facing FPV orientation on Starling 2. Verify against the actual vehicle's voxl-camera-server config and extrinsics calibration before using for georeferencing.
name: starling2_hires

fov_deg._note: Manufacturer-published values; vertical is omitted because the M0107 datasheet's listed value is inconsistent with horizontal/diagonal.

pinhole._note: Ideal-pinhole intrinsics derived from sensor + focal length. Useful for footprint projection and synthetic image generation. Real lens has barrel distortion; see distortion block.

distortion._note: Placeholder zeros. Replace with values from voxl-calibrate or OpenCV calibration of the specific unit.

mount_transform._description: Camera frame relative to vehicle body frame (FRD: +x forward, +y right, +z down). Translation in metres, rotation in degrees, applied as R_z(yaw) * R_y(pitch) * R_x(roll). Camera optical axis points along +x_camera.
mount_transform._caveat: Translation is a nominal estimate from Starling 2 photos; rotation assumes the FPV (forward) configuration. The Starling 2 also ships configs where the hires camera is angled down -- check the actual extrinsics for the unit in question.
